name: ai-api-guard
description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw AI-API calls when a sidecar CLI wraps the same operation, implemented in hexa-lang (`_ai_api_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Two deny patterns: (1) `curl`/`wget`/`http`/`httpie` to an AI hostname (`api.openai.com` · `api.anthropic.com` · `fal.run` · `api.replicate.com` · `generativelanguage.googleapis.com`); (2) inline `python(3)?` / `node` / `deno` / `bun` with `-c`/`-e` AND `import` of `openai` / `anthropic` / `fal_client` / `replicate` / `google.generativeai`. Redirects to `/imagine` (image) / `/research` (text) / etc. — commons @D g50 enforcement. General SDK usage in committed code files (no inline `-c`/`-e`) passes through. 0.2.0 — s14 capacity-relief detector added: a non-blocking advisory (additionalContext, NOT a deny) fires when a Bash command or Write/Edit content SETS/EXPORTS/WRITES an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (or wires `--api-key` for anthropic) as a Claude rate-limit/capacity workaround, redirecting to the SANCTIONED CLI routes (account swap via `/quota` · CLI routers · Bedrock/Vertex backends) per project.tape @D s14; matcher widened to Bash|Write|Edit for the s14 path. NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11).
